microtasks: Write a type-annotated function that accepts any iterable and returns a dict of type→count. Test with 5 different inputs.
Implement a calculator using operator overloading — no if/else chains. Use a dict mapping operator strings to lambda functions.
Build a grade classifier: given a list of scores, return a dict grouping names by letter grade. Use comprehension, not loops.
Implement FizzBuzz — then rewrite it 3 ways: loop, list comprehension, generator. Time each with timeit.
Build a closure-based counter factory: counter() returns a function that increments, resets, and reports. No global variables.
Build 3 decorators: @timer (logs execution time), @retry(n) (retries on exception), @cache (memoize results). Use @wraps on all.
Implement an infinite Fibonacci generator. Then build a sliding-window generator that yields (prev, current) pairs.
Build a DB connection context manager class and a @contextmanager function version. Handle exceptions in both.
Rewrite 5 nested-loop operations as single comprehensions. Benchmark the speedup with timeit.
Use itertools to solve: find all pairs in a list that sum to a target. Use functools.reduce to flatten a nested list.
Build a Vector2D class with __add__, __mul__, __abs__, __iter__, __repr__. Make it immutable using __slots__.
Implement a Singleton using __new__. Then implement it using a metaclass. Explain the difference in your INTEL entry.
Design a Shape hierarchy: abstract Shape → Circle, Rectangle, Triangle. Use @abstractmethod for area() and perimeter().
Build a Protocol-based Serializable interface. Make 3 classes implement it. Use isinstance() with Protocol to verify.
Create a custom exception hierarchy for an API client. Use logging with rotating file handler. Log all exceptions with traceback.
Visualize the event loop: instrument asyncio to print every task switch. Run 5 concurrent coroutines and trace execution order.
Rewrite a synchronous web scraper (5 URLs) as async with aiohttp. Compare wall-clock times. Log each request's latency.
Use asyncio.gather vs asyncio.TaskGroup to fetch 10 URLs concurrently. Handle individual failures without crashing the group.
Build an async rate-limiter using asyncio.Semaphore. Apply it to an API client capped at 5 requests/second.
Use ThreadPoolExecutor to run a CPU-bound function concurrently. Then use ProcessPoolExecutor. Explain which is faster and why.
resource: Real Python — Async IO in Python

microtasks: Build a login form handler that prevents SQL injection, XSS, and command injection. Use parameterized queries + bleach for HTML.
Audit an existing project for hardcoded secrets using truffleHog or similar. Replace with python-dotenv + os.environ pattern.
Hash a password with passlib (bcrypt). Implement a timing-safe comparison. Explain why == is dangerous for secret comparison.
Build a HTTPS client that pins the certificate, validates expiry, and logs the TLS version used. Reject anything < TLS 1.2.
Run pip-audit on a requirements.txt with intentionally outdated deps. Fix all critical CVEs. Document what each vulnerability was.
Implement a dynamic array from scratch in Python. Include resize logic. Benchmark append vs insert at index.
Build a doubly-linked list with O(1) insertAtHead, insertAtTail, deleteNode. Implement LRU Cache using it.
Implement a stack-based expression evaluator (handles +,-,*,/ and parentheses). Then implement a monotonic stack.
Build a HashMap from scratch using open addressing + linear probing. Handle load factor > 0.7 with resize.
Implement a min-heap from scratch. Build a heap-based median finder that updates in O(log n) per insertion.
Implement binary search on rotated sorted array. Then: find first/last position of element. No library. Explain loop invariants.
Implement merge sort and quicksort from scratch. Add instrumentation to count comparisons. Test on sorted, reversed, random arrays.
Build a BST with insert/delete/search. Add inorder/preorder/postorder traversal. Check if a BST is valid.
Implement BFS and DFS on adjacency list. Find shortest path (BFS). Detect cycle (DFS). Find connected components.
Solve: Longest Common Subsequence, 0/1 Knapsack, Coin Change — first with memoization, then convert to tabulation.
Take a God Class (500+ line class) and refactor it to satisfy all 5 SOLID principles. Document each violation you fixed.
Build a notification system using Factory pattern. Supports Email/SMS/Slack. Adding a new channel requires zero changes to existing code.
Implement Decorator pattern for an HTTP client: add logging, retry, and rate-limiting as composable decorators.
Build an event bus using Observer pattern. Publishers emit typed events. Multiple subscribers react independently. No direct coupling.
Refactor a tightly-coupled module using DI and an IOC container. Constructor injection only. Write unit tests — mock all dependencies.
Take a denormalized schema (users + orders + products in one table). Normalize it to 3NF. Justify every decomposition decision.
Simulate a bank transfer. Implement with BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK. Test all 4 ACID properties with deliberate failure injections.
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on a slow query. Add a composite index. Show before/after query plan. Explain each node in the plan.
Implement read replicas pattern: writes go to primary, reads distributed across replicas. Add a read preference strategy.
Configure pgBouncer in transaction mode. Load-test with 1000 concurrent connections. Show connection count before/after.
Design a MongoDB schema for a social feed (users, posts, likes, comments). Choose embed vs reference for each. Justify with query patterns.
Use Redis sorted sets to build a real-time leaderboard. Add ZADD/ZRANK/ZRANGE. Implement TTL-based expiry for time-windowed scores.
Implement cache-aside, write-through, and write-back patterns for a product catalog. Benchmark read latency for each.
Simulate cache stampede: 1000 concurrent requests for an expired key. Fix with probabilistic early expiration or lock-based refresh.
Design a Cassandra table for time-series IoT data. Choose partition key to avoid hot partitions. Run queries without ALLOW FILTERING.
resource: Redis University RU101 + MongoDB University M101

microtasks: Take an existing app Dockerfile. Reduce image size using multi-stage builds and .dockerignore. Target < 100MB from > 500MB.
Write a docker-compose.yml for a full stack: app + PostgreSQL + Redis + nginx. Health checks on all services. One command to start.
Deploy a stateless app to Kubernetes: write Deployment (3 replicas), Service (ClusterIP), and HorizontalPodAutoscaler. Test rollout.
Store a DB password as a Kubernetes Secret. Mount as env var AND as a file. Show that it is base64 in etcd (explain why this matters).
Harden a container: non-root user, read-only filesystem, drop ALL capabilities, add only NET_BIND_SERVICE. Verify with docker inspect.
Deploy a Lambda function triggered by S3 upload. It resizes images and saves to another bucket. Use IAM roles — no access keys.
Architect a serverless API: API Gateway + Lambda + DynamoDB. Draw the request lifecycle. Implement cold start mitigation.
Write Terraform to provision a VPC + EC2 + RDS. Use modules. Store state in S3 with DynamoDB locking. Apply and destroy cleanly.
resource: AWS Cloud Practitioner Essentials + HashiCorp Terraform Tutorials

microtasks: Set up a deliberately vulnerable app (DVWA). Exploit all OWASP Top 10 categories. Document each attack and fix in writing.
Write a raw SQL query with injection vulnerability. Exploit it. Then fix with parameterized queries. Test with sqlmap.
Build a comment system with stored XSS vulnerability. Exploit it. Then add CSP headers and input sanitization. Verify bypass is impossible.
Implement a state-transfer attack using CSRF on a form. Then fix with CSRF token + SameSite=Strict cookie. Test both.
Build an API with IDOR on /users/{id}/profile. Demonstrate unauthorized access. Fix with ownership check middleware.
Exploit a JWT with alg:none attack. Then exploit weak secret brute-force. Fix both. Implement refresh token rotation.
Implement full OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE flow from scratch. No library for the flow logic — only for HTTP calls.
Show why MD5 and SHA1 are broken for passwords. Implement bcrypt and argon2 hashing. Benchmark both under load.
Use openssl to inspect a TLS handshake. Identify: cipher suite, certificate chain, OCSP stapling. Explain what each field means.
Implement RSA encryption/decryption and AES-256-GCM (authenticated encryption). Explain when to use each and why.
Implement sliding-window rate limiter using Redis. Handles 100 req/min per IP. Returns Retry-After header on 429.
Build an API key rotation system: issue, rotate, revoke. Keys are hashed in DB (never stored plaintext). Test revocation is instant.
Audit a web app's HTTP response headers with securityheaders.com. Add all missing headers. Get an A+ grade.
Run truffleHog on a git repo to find leaked secrets. Then integrate git-secrets pre-commit hook. Document what was found.
Run OWASP ZAP active scan against a local app. Document every finding. Fix at least the High severity issues.
Train a logistic regression and random forest on the Titanic dataset. Compare accuracy. Explain the confusion matrix for each.
Plot ROC-AUC curves for 3 models on the same dataset. Explain when AUC > accuracy as a metric. Calculate F1 manually.
Take a raw dataset with missing values, categorical features, and outliers. Build a preprocessing pipeline. Document every decision.
Train a neural network until overfitting. Apply L2 regularization, dropout, and early stopping. Show learning curves for each.
Build a scikit-learn Pipeline with custom transformers. Make it serializable with joblib. Show inference on new data.
Implement backpropagation from scratch with numpy (no PyTorch). Train on XOR problem. Visualize weight updates per epoch.
Build a PyTorch training loop from scratch: forward pass, loss, backward, optimizer step. Train MNIST classifier to > 98% accuracy.
Build a CNN image classifier on CIFAR-10. Add batch normalization and dropout. Plot training/validation curves.
Fine-tune ResNet18 on a custom 5-class dataset with < 500 images. Use data augmentation. Achieve > 85% validation accuracy.
Track 10 experiment runs with MLflow: log params, metrics, and artifacts. Compare runs in the MLflow UI. Register the best model.
Build a multi-turn chat assistant using an LLM API. Implement system prompt engineering for a specific persona. Compare 5 prompt variations.
Build a RAG pipeline: ingest 10 PDF documents → chunk → embed → store in ChromaDB → query with semantic search → generate answer.
Build a LangChain agent with 3 tools: web search, calculator, code interpreter. Handle tool errors gracefully.
Generate embeddings for 1000 product descriptions. Build a semantic similarity search. Compare cosine vs dot product similarity.
Read the QLoRA paper. Explain: what is quantization, what is LoRA, why QLoRA combines them. Write a 500-word technical summary.
resource: HuggingFace NLP Course + Generative AI Fundamentals

topics: State Management (Zustand vs Context vs Jotai)
microtasks: Build a useDebounce, useThrottle, usePrevious, and useDeepCompare custom hook. Write tests for each with React Testing Library.
Extract all business logic from a 300-line component into custom hooks. The component should be < 50 lines, only JSX.
Build the same feature with Zustand, Context API, and Jotai. Benchmark re-renders with React DevTools Profiler. Document tradeoffs.
Profile a slow list component. Identify unnecessary re-renders. Fix with React.memo, useMemo, useCallback. Show before/after flame graph.
Add Error Boundary to a component tree. Implement Suspense with lazy-loaded routes. Add React Query for async state with loading/error states.
resource: React Docs — Hooks Reference + Kent C. Dodds Blog

topics: App Router: RSC, Server Actions, Streaming
microtasks: Build a page using RSC for data fetching, Server Actions for mutations, and Streaming with Suspense. Zero client-side data fetching.
Build an API route with middleware that: validates JWT, rate-limits per user, logs to DB. Chain middleware cleanly.
Implement NextAuth with Google OAuth. Add role-based access control (admin/user). Protect routes at middleware level.
Instrument a Next.js app: add fetch() caching headers, use unstable_cache for DB queries, trigger revalidatePath on mutation.
Deploy to Vercel Edge Runtime. Measure TTFB improvement vs Node.js runtime. Document what code cannot run on Edge and why.
Build a type-safe API client using generics: fetchAPI<ResponseType>(url) with full TypeScript inference. No any, no as casting.
Implement DeepReadonly<T>, DeepPartial<T>, and a type-safe EventEmitter<Events> from scratch using conditional types.
Run Lighthouse on a production app. Score < 70 on LCP. Fix: optimize images, add preload hints, defer non-critical JS. Score > 90.
Use webpack-bundle-analyzer on a Next.js app. Find the 3 largest modules. Replace/tree-shake 2 of them. Reduce bundle > 30%.
Move a CPU-intensive operation (large array sort + transform) to a Web Worker. Show main thread stays unblocked during processing.
resource: web.dev — Core Web Vitals + TypeScript Handbook

topics: Message Queues: Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs SQS
API Design: REST vs GraphQL vs gRPC
microtasks: Design a distributed key-value store. Explain every CAP tradeoff. Draw the architecture. Choose consistency model and defend it.
Design a URL shortener that handles 100K writes/day and 10M reads/day. Include load balancer, CDN, DB sharding strategy.
Compare Kafka vs RabbitMQ for 3 scenarios: event streaming, task queue, pub-sub. For each: choose a winner and explain why.
Design a public API: choose REST vs GraphQL vs gRPC for a social platform, a payment processor, and an IoT data ingestion service.
Implement token bucket rate limiter in Redis. Add circuit breaker with three states (closed/open/half-open). Handle failure cascades.
resource: System Design Primer (GitHub) + Designing Data-Intensive Applications

microtasks: Design Twitter's feed: fanout-on-write vs fanout-on-read. Handle celebrities (10M followers). Cover timeline generation at scale.
Design a distributed cache layer for a read-heavy app. Handle eviction policies, consistent hashing, and node failure.
Design a payment system: handle idempotency, double-spend prevention, exactly-once processing, and PCI compliance constraints.
Add OpenTelemetry to an existing app: structured logs, custom metrics, distributed traces. Build a Grafana dashboard with 5 key SLIs.
resource: Grokking the System Design Interview + High Scalability Blog

topics: Go Basics: Types, Functions, Structs, Interfaces
microtasks: Implement a type-safe stack and queue in Go using generics (Go 1.18+). Write table-driven tests. Use go vet and golangci-lint.
Build a pipeline using goroutines and channels: generator → transform → sink. Add fan-out and fan-in. Prevent goroutine leaks.
Implement Go's error wrapping pattern: errors.Is, errors.As, %w. Build a custom error type with stack trace. Never use panic for flow control.
Build a Go HTTP server with middleware chain: logging, authentication, CORS, timeout. Use chi or stdlib mux. Add graceful shutdown.
Write unit tests for all above. Use testify. Add a benchmark test. Generate coverage report. Target > 80% coverage.
resource: Go by Example + Tour of Go (Official)

microtasks: Implement the Saga pattern for an order fulfillment system: choreography vs orchestration. Handle rollback for 3 failure scenarios.
Build a CQRS system: separate write model (commands) from read model (projections). Rebuild read model from event log.
Implement consistent hashing with virtual nodes. Add and remove nodes. Show key distribution before/after. Visualize with a chart.
Explain Rust's ownership rules by building a stack data structure. Demonstrate: move semantics, clone cost, borrow checker errors and fixes.
Add explicit lifetime annotations to 5 functions. Build a struct with a lifetime parameter. Make the borrow checker happy without cloning.
Implement a generic Repository<T: Serialize + Deserialize> trait. Use it for 3 different models.
Build an async TCP server with tokio that handles 1000 concurrent connections. Add timeout on each connection with tokio::time::timeout.
Build a CLI tool with proper error propagation using thiserror and anyhow. Every error has context. No unwrap() in library code.
resource: The Rust Programming Language (The Book) + Rustlings

topics: Data Pipelines with Apache Kafka Streams
microtasks: Build a Kafka Streams topology: ingest events, filter, transform, aggregate in 1-minute windows, output to new topic.
Process a 1GB CSV with PySpark: clean nulls, join with a lookup table, aggregate by 3 dimensions, write to Parquet.
Build an Airflow DAG: extract from API → transform with Pandas → load to PostgreSQL. Add retry logic and email on failure.
Write a dbt model that transforms raw orders into a clean orders_summary. Add tests: not_null, unique, accepted_values.
Add Great Expectations to a pipeline. Define 5 expectations on a dataset. Fail the pipeline if expectations are not met.
Instrument a Python/Go app with Prometheus metrics: counters, histograms, gauges. Add /metrics endpoint. Write an alerting rule.
Build a Grafana dashboard: request rate, error rate, p50/p95/p99 latency, saturation. The USE method for every resource.
Add OpenTelemetry tracing to a 3-service system. Show end-to-end trace in Jaeger. Identify the slowest span.
Ship structured logs from an app to Loki. Build a Grafana Explore query that finds all 5xx errors in the last hour.
Define SLIs for an API (availability + latency). Set SLO targets. Calculate error budget. Simulate a budget-burning incident.
resource: Datadog Learning Center + Google SRE Book

microtasks: Refactor a synchronous API into event-driven: producer emits events, multiple consumers react independently. Show decoupling benefit.
Build a full GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline: lint → test → build Docker image → push to registry → deploy to staging → smoke test.
Implement blue-green deployment for a stateless app. Automate the switch with health checks. Roll back in < 30 seconds on failure.
Write a blameless postmortem for a real or simulated incident. Use the 5-Whys technique. Define 3 actionable follow-up items.
Audit a codebase for technical debt. Score it using SQALE model. Build a 4-week pay-down plan. Prioritize by ROI.
resource: GitHub Actions Official Docs + Google SRE Workbook

microtasks: Write a README for your best project: problem statement, architecture diagram, tech stack rationale, known limitations, future work.
Record a 5-minute demo of your strongest project. No filler words. Explain the hardest technical decision you made and why.
